channel.title: Reverb — New Bass Guitars
channel.description: Recently listed bass guitars on Reverb

item.title: 【new】Sadowsky / MX20 UV4 OWT/R 3.9kg #SMX A 000155-26【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,449
item.description:
$1,449 · Excellent · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Sadowsky is a high-end New York brand with top-tier musicians such as Marcus Miller and Will Lee as clients. (Titles omitted)

Previously, the Metroline Express was manufactured in Japan, but as Warwick took over the distribution of Sadowsky products, manufacturing shifted to a factory in China.

While keeping the selling price down, high quality is maintained, making it a very unique product in its price range.

This is the MX20 UV4, the first PB-type guitar in the MX series.

The body is made of lightweight solid Okoume, the neck is made of Roasted Maple, and the fingerboard is made of Morado (Pau Ferro).

This is a standard combination in the MX series.

The construction has been thoroughly considered for excellent body balance and sound projection, resulting in a very refined balance that is comfortable to hold.

The back of the neck has a satin finish, providing a smooth and pleasant feel.

The pickup is a "Sadowsky passive P-Style pickup."

It has the powerful, forceful projection characteristic of PB-type basses, yet delivers a clear sound without muddy bass tones.

The preamp features the familiar 2-band EQ.

You can significantly boost the bass and treble frequencies with the touch of a button.

The circuit board of this preamp is almost identical to that of the top-of-the-line models priced around 1 million yen.

Copper foil shielding is also applied, resulting in surprisingly low noise.

These points are extremely significant advantages when choosing an MX series bass.

Even in this price range, Roger Sadowsky's uncompromising commitment to instrument making is evident.

While offering the ultra-smooth playability characteristic of Sadowsky, the sound is unmistakably that of a P-bass.

It outputs a modern tone that beautifully blends a robust punch with sweet, airy, and clear treble tones.

Based on the traditional PB design, this guitar incorporates modern elements, embodying Roger Sadowsky's philosophy.

SPECS

Scale Length: 34" (864 mm) Long Scale
Body Shape: P-Style body
Body Wood: Okoume, Solidbody
Neck Wood: Roasted Maple (C-Shape, Bolt-On)
Fingerboard Wood: Morado
Inlays: White dot inlays
Fingerboard Radius: 12"
Nut: Sadowsky Just-A-Nut III
Nut Width: 1.5" (38.5 mm)
Nut String Spacing: Nut string spacing (all strings): 1.22" / 31 mm
Neck width at 12th fret: 2.22" / 56.3 mm neck width at 12th fret
Neck width at last fret: 2.59" / 65.7 mm neck width at last fret
Neck thickness at 1st fret: 0.82" / 20.8 mm neck thickness at 1st fret
Neck thickness at 12th fret: 0.96" / 24.6 mm neck thickness at 12th fret
# Frets: 20 Nickel Silver (width: 0.094" (2.4 mm) / height: 0.051" (1.3 mm))
Neck Pickup: Sadowsky passive P-Style pickup
Bridge Pickup: None
Preamp: Active Sadowsky 2-way electronics (Bass and Treble boost)
Controls: Volume / Treble / Bass
Push/Pull Function: Push/pull function at Bass knob for preamp bypass
Machine Heads: Sadowsky Light Open Gear Machine Heads
Security Locks: Sadowsky S-Security Locks
Bridge: Sadowsky bridge with quick string release
Bridge Total String Spacing: 2.24" / 57mm
Hardware Color: Chrome
Strings: Sadowsky Silver Label, Stainless Steel, 045 - 105
Protector: Sadowsky Fretboard Protector
Tools: Hex key tools
Bag / Case: Sadowsky PortaBag
Made In: China

Accessories: Original gig bag, warranty card

Text: Kaigo Kawami

Product photos: Muragishi

Manufacturer's warranty included

(Warranty is only valid for purchases made in Japan)

●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee
Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.

If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.

Note: All electronic devices, such as effect pedals and amplifiers, are set for use in Japan.
Please be sure to check the voltage and power specifications of your country before purchasing and using.

item.title: Rickenbacker 4003S/5 2021 - Fireglo — $2,300
item.description:
$2,300 · Very Good · Curtis' Corner

For Sale is my 2021 Rickenbacker 4003S/ 5 string bass guitar. Item is listed in very good condition, has been lightly played a handful of times over the last few years. There is a small indentation near the bottom of the guitar as shown in the photos. Couldn’t tell if that’s how the wood naturally contours or if it was bumped along the way, price reflects that potential blemish. All other wear is from light use. Plays and functions excellent. Item comes with OHSC. Feel free to ask any questions or see further photos

item.title: G&L SB1 Lined Fretless 2021 - Sunburst Frost — $2,100
item.description:
$2,100 · Excellent · VincentYGB

2021 Sunburst Frost with black headstock, gorgeous lined ebony fingerboard, natural satin neck feels perfect your hand. Rare 1 1/2" nut and before the GuitarsbyLeo Registry stopped working, was the latest SB1 fretless bass so may be the last SB1 fretless made. Tone for days and super lightweight at 8 lbs., 5 oz. 9.5 radius. $400 in upgrades from stock. Most would call this mint condition but I find it tough to call most black pickguards mint. Great condition TKL hardshell tolex case. If I were still gigging I'd keep this one but have a '93 SB1 unlined fretless, also with 1 1/2' nut and it's been with me a long time. These two were the only jazz-necked SB1's listed on the registry. No build sheet or COA- I had them and have looked everywhere so can't promise I'll find them.

More US G&L basses and guitars coming.

Listed here first but will be cross-listed online & locally so first jump lands her.

item.title: Upgraded McGrath Thunderhorse IV with Dunable Pickups 2025 - Turquoise—satin — $2,000
item.description:
$2,000 · Excellent · Marshall’s Bass Base

***Please read*** As of 6/30/26, I’ve run out of bass shilling boxes—more will arrive in three days, so **please note** that if you purchase this item it will ship after a few days, free original gig bag included.

Barely used, never gigged with McGrath Thunder horse IV that’s lived in my climate controlled professional home studio for about a year, played infrequently only because I have too many options, so I’m selling er’ away to someone who will use it as much as it deserves. Cool bass with a push-pull mute on the D-Bird volume knob.


Neck: Dunable D-Bird

Middle: Split P

Bridge: Dunable Jazz

Sounds really great. 3 vol, 1 tone. All on at the same time sounds super rad, rare to find this configuration.


Full hipshot hardware. Dunlop strap locks.

I installed a non-matching gold drop tuner, and will include the marching regular Hipshot tuner with the bass.

Will ship fully insured in a gig bag at no extra cost, professionally packed by an experienced guitar/bass packer and shipper.

item.title: G&L CLF Research L-2500 Series 750 5 String Bass - Blue Burst, Maple Fingerboard, Original Case — $1,850
item.description:
$1,850 · Excellent · Lyons Den Music

Up for grabs is my beloved G&L CLF Research L-2500 Series 750 5-String Bass in Blue Burst with a gorgeous maple neck and fairly new set of Thomastik-Infeld JF345 Jazz Flatwound Bass Strings (Long Scale .043-.136). Includes original case (which has some minor surface scuffs), truss rod keys, certificate of authenticity and spec sheet. Bass is in mint condition and I can't find any visible flaws. Active electronics work perfectly offering loads of flexibility for any genre. It was purchased new in Sept 2024 and has only been gigged a few times locally.

Learn more at: glguitars.com/product/clf-research-l-2500-series-750/

item.title: Marleaux Consat 6 string bass 2017 — $4,650
item.description:
$4,650 · Excellent · The Gear Is Here

Here’s my Marleaux Consat 6 string bass in a stunning aqua flame finish.
This beast is as slick as they come. By far the best 6 string I’ve ever owned.
Bass is in amazing condition, almost new. There’s no cosmetic issues I can see.
Electronics work perfectly.
The action is sweet and low. No bad spots in the neck. The neck is nice and thin, the weight is not bad at all, no neck dive, balanced perfectly. It plays tight and buttery.
Specs-


MINT!

Model Consat Custom

Construction Bolt On

Build Date January 2017

Serial Number 2040

Number of Strings 6

Woods

Body Mahogany

Top Maple Figures

Neck Mape

Fingerboard Ebony

Special Signature Top

Hardware

Bridge ETS Gold

Knobs ETS Gold, added custom abalone and gold knobs

Tuners Schaller Gold

Electronics

Pickups Delano SBC6HES4 with coil tap each pickup

EQ Marleaux BC3

Finish Satin

Color Aquamarine

Special Oval Inlays

Sold as is, no returns.
No trades, thanks.
Lowball offers will be ignored.
Ships to the CONTUS only, UPS ground.
Cheers!

item.title: RKS SymBass 5string “The Green Manalishi” — $2,800
item.description:
$2,800 · Excellent · The Gear Is Here

What we have here is my ultra rare RKS Symbass that was only built for 2 years.
It is named “The Green Manalishi”
This is a very interesting and unique bass. I’ve seen only one other.
This was designed for the back body to be removed and swapped out with other available RKS bodies….different styles, finishes etc.

Unfortunately the previous owner informed me the body is permanently attached. I didn't mind as I found it perfect as is.

This bass is in excellent condition and hasn't been played much.
There’s some tiny dings, a surface scratch or two, and a scuff or two that I’ve pointed out in the pics.
The fret ends could be smoothed out a bit to your personal preference, I found them a bit sharp.
The action is sweet and low. The neck plays fast and smooth.

It comes with:

  1. ﻿﻿﻿the original hard shell case
  2. ﻿﻿﻿certificate of authenticity
  3. ﻿﻿﻿adjustment tools

I have replaced the original Bridge with a direct replacement Hipshot A style Bridge.

(Because the original Bridge didn't go low enough to lower the action).

I also installed copper shielding tape because I was getting radio stations and feedback. That's fixed.

The original bridge is in the glove box of the case, inside of the bag. This bass weighs 10

Ib 2 oz.

The pickup is very powerful and doesn't require much volume.

I currently have D'Addario NYXL 45 to 135 strings on it.

So, It you like the sound of a Stingray, this is your animal. And no batteries to buy.

This instrument is passive and only has a volume and a tone. Nice and simple.

It is perfectly balanced and has flame Maple body and neck. Has an Ebony fretboard with Abalone inlays.

Neck is nice and thin, action is medium/low.

***This is being sold as is, no returns.

Offers considered. Lowball offers will be ignored. No trades thank you.

Ships in an almost new HS case to the CONTUS only UPS ground.
Cheers!

item.title: Sterling Ray 5 HH — $355
item.description:
$355 · Good · String, Things, and BaDa-Bing!

I got this in on a trade. It's in excellent condition and hardly played. Includes the soft case. The case has seen some action (see pic) but no issues.

item.title: Charvel Explorer Bass Zebra Graphic 1984 w/OHSC — $3,499.99
item.description:
$3,499.99 · Good · DIABLO GUITARS - AXES OF EVIL!

Vintage Charvel Explorer Bass in Zebra Graphic finish made in 1984 with Original Hard Shell Case. This guitar features a solid poplar body, maple neck and rosewood fingerboard. Equipped with its original Charvel P & J-Style pickups in the middle & bridge positions and controlled by 2 volume pots & 1 tone pot. The 34" scale length neck has clay dot inlays and white side markers with 21 jumbo frets on a 16" radius with a medium "C" profile. The hardware is comprised of black Charvel Open Gear tuning machines with Y-Shape heads, a graphite nut, a Charvel 4-Saddle Adjustable bridge and gold knurled dome knobs.

This guitar is in overall good condition. There are small dings, dents and light surface marks throughout the guitar. There is discoloration on the back of the neck. The neck pocket has a couple of small finish splits in it. There is light delamination of the finish on the headstock. The plating on the knobs is mostly worn off. The truss rod cover is missing. (See Pics)

Guitar is freshly setup and restrung with 45-100 strings. Includes Original Hard Shell Case.

  • Model: Explorer Bass

  • Condition: Good

item.title: Moon JB-5A - Snow White — $2,204.88
item.description:
$2,204.88 · Very Good · Okanagan Guitar Gear

An amazing bass handmade in Japan. No noticable damage outside of a small chip on the back. Easily the best feeling and sounding bass I've ever played I just don't have a need for a 5-string. Feel free to message me if you have any questions!

Moon JB-5A bass

Body: Alder 2pc

Neck: Maple

Fingerboard: Ebony

Frets: 21

Scale: 34 inch

Tuners: Gotoh GB11W

Bridge: Gotoh 404BO-5

Colour: Snow White

Finish: Urethane

Pickups: Oxalis JB5 pickups wired into Bartolini XTCT preamp circuit

Case: Original gig case

Weight: 4.47 kg

Manufacturer's description:

The JB-5 was born from a single idea: wanting a Low-B note in a traditional Jazz Bass. Its

ideas and sound were realized through extensive discussions with numerous players. Every detail, from the design, mass, and weight of the parts, including the 5-hole control plate, 2-volume, and 3-band EQ, has been carefully considered. Its timeless style makes it easy for even first-time 5-string bass players to play without feeling out of place.

We want the JB-5 to become the standard for 5-string basses.

item.description:
$5,001.44 · Excellent · Roman 's Gear Emporium

item.description:
$700 · Very Good · Supafingga Musical Instruments

* Ash body * Maple neck * Rosewood fretboard * 25 frets * Brass nut * Chrome saddles and tuners.  One of the outer plastic stems apoears to be non-original (please see circled part of pic no. 5)

item.title: Ernie Ball Music Man Stingray Special BFR 5HH 2025 - Lignite — $4,200
item.description:
$4,200 · Excellent · David Naranjo

This is a brand new mint condition Ernie Ball Stingray Special BFR 5HH Lignite limited to Sweetwater and no longer available. Only 20 of these beauties were made for 2025. This is #7 of #20. I feel like I got one with some very nice Flamed Maple Figuring. This one comes with Certificate Of Authenticity. This bass has only been tested to make sure it was all in working order other than that it has not been played and will just sit in its mono case until someone purchases it!

item.title: Fano Standard JM4 — $2,000
item.description:
$2,000 · Good · Kyle's Shop

This is the 8th Fano JM4 Standard built in 2016. I am the 2nd owner. This has been my main bass over the last several years and has a bit of wear. The playability and tone on this bass are unmatched in my opinion. Alder body, maple neck. Proprietary Fano single coil pickups. Volume, tone, tone. Passive. I hate to let it go but it seems like the proper move. The next owner will be quite happy!

item.title: Sire Marcus Miller V7 Alder 5 String Bright Metallic Red Electric Bass Guitar - Bright Metallic Red — $600
item.description:
$600 · Good · Alec's Gear

Marcus Miller Sire V7 5 String Bass in Metallic Red.

Very beautiful and awesome bass, I’m just looking to downsize to a smaller scale 4 string bass as I have smaller hands and make less heavier music nowadays.

Has a very small nick in the back of the neck.

item.title: Lakland Skyline 55-02 Deluxe Bass Guitar-Sweetwater Exclusive 2021 - Transparent Black — $1,599
item.description:
$1,599 · Excellent · Steve's Toys

I live not far from Sweetwater Music in Fort Wayne which is kind of cool...and kind of not.

Over the years too many "things" have followed me home, creating a collection of "things" that I probably shouldn't have purchased in the first place (just ask my wife, she'll tell you).

Anyway, as I'm getting older I've decided to do some stable cleaning. This bass was used occasionally at Xperience Church in Defiance, OH. With its Lakland pickups wound in Chicago, translucent black color exclusively sold at Sweetwater, and African Ebony fretboard, it an amazing looking and sounding instrument. Its action is low and it is an absolute joy to play and hear.

I went over it prior to listing and could find only a few extremely superficial scratches on its back. I tried, but they cannot be picked up by my camera. Most guitars on a showroom floor have more imperfections than this "thing." It is as close to new condition as you could ever imagine. I can include evidence that I purchased this bass guitar from Sweetwater Music on October 20th, 2021

item.title: 2024 BC Rich Warlock US Custom Shop B.C. Rich - Coffin Case OHSC — $2,200
item.description:
$2,200 · Excellent · Nick K's Bass Gear

This is a US Custom shop BC Rich Warlock bass that was finished and completed in 2024 by former BC Rich luthier Glen Graff. It is not an official Neal Moser custom shop bass like you would pay $7k for today but it started life as a custom shop US Warlock bass in 2011 when Bernie Rico Jr still owned the company, and sat unfinished in a storage locker for 13 years before then, so for all intents and purposes it was and is a US custom shop bass. In my opinion

This is everything you could want from a warlock bass. I rewired this bass with two used Dimarzio model P pickups, 500k pots with no load tones and push pull kill switch volume pots. You can find a wiring diagram I drew inside the cavity. I also added the correct Grover mini style tuners and recessed the dunlop strap locks into the bass- the upper one sticks into your chest when sitting down otherwise. I custom fitted the foam in the coffin case so that the bass is immobilized during travel, keeping the headstock point from recieving any stress while the case is closed. I would bet money you could throw this down the stairs in this case without a scratch.

The finish is very glossy, I believe it is a flat black base with like a ton of clear coats on top. Absolutely stunning in person. It also has a LOT of finish checking on the body going between the body points that is nearly impossible to photograph. It's only visible a few feet away- look at the reflections in the pictures for the image distortions. The black finish also has scratches from being used. Even taking these pictures I was leaving fingerprints and had to wipe it off before every shot. The points are all intact, theres some very slight dings on the upper treble side horn. That is also inevitable with pointy basses

Only selling for financial reasons. This asking price is FAR below what I have into this bass and still less than a third of what this would cost new from the current BCR custom shop. I asked them about what an ironbird bass would cost and was told roughly $7k. Or go look for another US made neck through BCR bass and see how impossible to find they are.

Willing to ship worldwide, be aware of potential import duties and fees
Please message me with any further questions
Thank you

* Please be aware that humidity, temperature, and handling can affect guitars & basses such that adjustments to the instrument might be needed post-shipment. This includes "fret sprout" (as wood can shrink/swell during shipment, a fretted instrument may arrive with sharp fret edges). If tweaking action, intonation, etc., having fret edges dressed, or having the instrument set up to your specs is not something you are comfortable with doing yourself, you will need the help of a local-to-you tech/professional. Note that any associated fees incurred for their time and labor will be your responsibility.

Please contact us to discuss any other questions you may have about it before clicking the "Add To Cart" button!


Atomic Music store hours:  12pm - 6pm, EST, Monday - Saturday & 12pm - 5pm, EST, Sundays.

Thanks!!

- Atomic Music

item.title: Schecter Stiletto Custom-5 Active 5-String Bass 2010s Natural Satin — $529
item.description:
$529 · Very Good · The Nerd Bunker

Are you a slap-happy funk metal maestro ready to make the leap to a fifth string? Are you a prog-jazz aficionado but don’t quite make country-club gig salary for that John Myung six-string Bongo, but want just a little more range? Are you a two-hand tapper who is not quite ready to sell off your first AND second-born child for a yin-yang shaped Fodera. Are you all of those and more?


Well I got the bass for you. This striking Schecter Stiletto Custom 5 is ready to rise to the challenge and more. Featuring brushed gold hardware and an aged flamed top, the finish looks like golden butter with an action and neck to match. These appointments say “classy mother****er” but this tone is pure shred. Featuring an active preamp and a pair of soapbar humbuckers, it can breathe fire or sing a lullaby—even in the same song.

But it’s not made to be a showpiece, as shown it has weathered a bit of belt rash and a couple dings that it wears like combat medals (see pictures), but the cosmetic wear is subtle and offset by the natural finish.

This has sat mostly unplayed since I stopped gigging many moons ago and so it needs a new forever home where it will be fully appreciated like the stallion it is.

item.title: Fender Precision Bass Fretless 1977 - Natural — $2,800
item.description:
$2,800 · Very Good · Matt Wiles

This is a lovely all-original 1977 fretless Fender Precision. I'm the third owner of this bass, and the person I acquired it from had it since 1980. This bass is on the lighter side at just 8 lbs, 14 oz.

The rosewood fingerboard is absolutely flawless, and contributes to the perfect low setup with no uneven spots. The truss rod is fully functional, and the neck is exactly where it should be.

The pickguard has a few warped areas that don't affect fitment. The original poly finish is in wonderful shape minus a small handful of cracks and scuffs due to being almost 50 years old.

The OHSC is in wonderful shape both inside and out. All latches work, the handle is securely attached, and the red plush interior is stain-free with no tears or peeling.

The original pickup and bridge covers are included. The original foam from the bridge cover is long gone. The thumb rest is the only "missing piece".

It kind of pains me to let this one go, but I don't play it enough to justify keeping it. This one needs to be loved and played. It feels too good to not be on stage on a regular basis.

item.title: Redeemer Basses BassMan StankRay 2026 - Purple Reign — $3,150
item.description:
$3,150 · Brand New · Redeemer Bass Guitars

The High-Headroom Funk Machine: The Redeemer Bass Man StankRay Evolution

Up for sale is a breathtaking, freshly completed, hand-constructed 2026 Redeemer Basses "Bass Man StankRay" 4-string bass. This instrument is a bold, high-performance reimagining of an iconic dual-cutaway humbucker legend. Pairing a deep-resonating Mahogany body, a mesmerizing custom color-shifting pearl finish, and elite Nordstrand electronics fueled by our ground-breaking onboard power engineering, it guarantees you'll be able to PUT A LIL STANK ON IT!

Mind-Bending Visuals & Premium Tone Woods

The body is carved from premium, punchy Mahogany and finished in our custom Purple Reign Pearlescent Flake. The paint architecture is incredible: it starts with an electric blue base, covered with four different shades of purple pearl and one shade of red pearl for a massive, color-shifting depth. All of this is topped with a heavy mix of blue, purple, and gold holographic flake, then buried under a flawless, high-gloss 2K urethane clear coat.

The 34" scale bolt-on maple neck features a satin smooth finish. It is paired with a maple fingerboard sporting custom purple pearl marker dots and a rock-solid dual-action truss rod. At the top of the neck sits a premium 43mm brass nut, giving you that classic, commanding width along with incredible open-string response and clarity for anchoring down hard-grooving stank basslines.

Massaged Heel Joint for Upper Fret Access

Forget the bulky, blocky heel joints of traditional factory builds. This bass features a meticulously massaged and smoothed neck-to-body joint, completely eliminating the standard corner obstruction. Your hand transitions seamlessly into the higher registers with maximum palm comfort—giving you total freedom to execute fast fills all the way up the neck.

Premium Nordstrand Electronics & Pro Controls

The StankRay is engineered to hit with a massive, focused midrange punch and thunderous lows. It is loaded with a premium Nordstrand MM Humbucker, meticulously designed to capture the raw power, snap, and growl of the classic '70s music machines, but with modern clarity and hum-free performance.

The pickup runs into an elite Nordstrand 3-band active preamp, providing legendary, ultra-musical tone-shaping capabilities. It features a mid-switch and an active passive switch. Fully shielded cavities ensure that your signal stays perfectly clean and free of undesirable venue RF noise or stage hum.

Redeemer 16V Smart Power System

No more hunting for 9V batteries or losing headroom when you dig into a heavy STANK groove! This bass features our proprietary Redeemer 16V 4S Lithium-Ion battery system. Need to charge on the go? It uses the exact same USB-C charger as any modern cell phone.

  • Massive Headroom: Running the active Nordstrand preamp with a max charge of 16.8V delivers incredible dynamic range, letting that MM humbucker snap and punch with absolute authority without clipping.
  • USB-C Rechargeable: Fully integrated charging port—just plug it in between sets or at home. You can expect a massive play time of approximately 500 hours between charges, meaning you rarely ever have to plug it in!
  • In a hurry? No time to charge? Don't worry, just flip the mini-toggle active/passive switch and rock on!
  • LED Status Array: A flush 4-LED status indicator gives you a crystal-clear look at your current power levels at the press of a button, along with a dedicated charging status LED so you know exactly when you're topped off.

Dependable, Premium Hipshot Hardware

The hardware features an ultra-sleek, custom chrome and black mix throughout. It is anchored by a high-mass Hipshot Mass Kicker Bridge for incredible string anchoring, deep resonance, and endless sustain. This is paired with world-class Hipshot HB-1 Tuners to guarantee flawless tuning stability and total reliability on stage.

Condition & Accessories

This bass has been fully set up, intonated, and bench-tested. It plays like butter with low action and zero buzz. It ships fully charged and ready to command the stage or studio.

  • Strings: Equipped with a brand new set of premium D'Addario XL Pro Steel Strings.
  • Strap Locks: Comes equipped with professional strap locks to protect your baby from accidents!
  • Case: A padded gig bag is included in the sale.
  • Tool Kit: The Allen keys required for truss rod and bridge adjustments are included.

About Redeemer Bass Guitars

Behind every Redeemer Bass is Scott Coleman Sr, a solo luthier who has been hand-crafting elite bass guitars since 2005. Hand-built in Central Texas, USA, Redeemer Basses operates strictly on a low-scale, high-concept level—meaning mass production does not exist here. Every single build is a true "one-off" masterpiece, completely unique from anything that came before it.

Because production is strictly limited, owning a Redeemer Bass means owning a rare piece of functional art. Scott dedicates uncompromised time to the critical details that define a world-class instrument: Custom designs and options, single-cuts, dual-cuts, neck-throughs, bolt-ons, precision fretwork for ultra-low action, immaculate custom finishes, advanced electronic integration, and a rigorous bench setup.

While Scott has the engineering expertise to design and produce his own proprietary Redeemer-branded electronics and hardware systems, he also proudly teams up with the most respected names in the bass community—including Aguilar Amplification, Nordstrand Audio, Bartolini Pickups & Electronics, and Hipshot Products. This unique blend of custom shop innovation and industry-standard elite components ensures you get the exact tone, feel, and performance you desire. When you buy a Redeemer, you are buying a bespoke instrument engineered to perform flawlessly for a lifetime.

item.title: Solano Speed Shop 969GT 2026 - Black Figured Maple Top — $2,600
item.description:
$2,600 · Brand New · Solano Speed Shop

This is the second of three 969GT basses being crafted this year.

This unit features a figured maple top with a Canarywood strip up the middle, and black maple purfling separating the Canarywood. The Maple has been stained with a silvery/black stain which will look more black or silver depending on the angle you view it from. It's a simple but beautiful top, and the maple has a natural sheen that works well with this color. The figure Maple top is paired with a Genuine Mahogany body and uses maple and black veneer layer to cleanly isolate the top. Electronics cover is the same figured maple used for the top. Currently it is natural, but can be matched to the front if you prefer.

This body also features a generous comfort carve but lacks a forearm carve. I preferred the look of the uninterrupted top, and used a larger radii for the edge in that area. Still very comfortable without breaking the view of the maple top.

The neck is a Nine piece with Maple and Padouk. Visually striking and very solid. Featuring a double adjustable spoke wheel truss rod with a pair of carbon rods for added stability.

The fretboard contains 24 StewMac 152G frets (Medium Higher profile: .048 fret height) in their gold fret wire. Looks great against the Pale Moon Ebony board and gold mother of pearl dots. I marked the 12th fret with a single gold mop square to leave as much of the natural figuring of the pale moon ebony visible along the board. I used clear flamed maple to bind the fretboard.

The headstock shows off the neck wood stack up, and uses Hipshot Lightweight tuning machines with clover leaf tuners. A string retainer ensures solid contact with the solid Brass nut. Nut width is 1.5", and string spacing at the bridge is 19mm (standard).

The bridge is a solid brass piece, also from StewMac.

The pickups are the Fishman Fluence soapbar set with three voicings on tap via a three way switch, as well as a coil split feature, accessed by pulling up on the volume knob. The Fluence set includes a 2 band preamp so you can dial in exactly what you need. This set sounds incredible with these woods. Bright and articulate, with control when you need it. Control knobs are solid ebony with brass ringed turquoise position indicators.

All of my basses and guitars from 2025 onward feature Pure Tone jacks for Stereo (active) and mono setups. I find these jacks to be superior in every way to others that I have used, and appreciate how well they "hold" the cable securely.

I use 5mm bolts for all of my basses, and this one is no different. This one includes gold ferrules and stainless steel conical seats for the neck bolts which sit beautifully against the Mahogany body.

item.title: Epiphone Grabber Bass Reissue 1998 - Blonde — $999.99
item.description:

This used item is located at

item.title: Peavey Milestone IV — $149.99
item.description:
$149.99 · Fair · Music Go Round - St Paul

Cosmetically, this bass is in good to very good condition. The front of the body has a few light scratches, and the back has more--particularly around the bottom edge. The pickguard is warping slightly.

The reason why we are calling it fair is because this buzz has an electronic buzz to it. This bass will need a little love before its playing at its best. Truss rod, bridge, saddles, and machine heads all work great.

item.title: Solano Speed Shop 969GT 2026 - Canarywood Topped Jazz Bass — $2,300
item.description:
$2,300 · Brand New · Solano Speed Shop

This is the first of three 969GT basses coming out this year. This one-of-one bass features a slightly offset body with generous comfort and forearm carves. The body is Mahogany, and the top is spalted Canarywood. There are two veneer layers separating the top from the body. Body weight is approximately 8lbs. (sorry - my scale needs replacing - it's on the way!).

The neck is a Thirteen piece with figured Roasted maple with maple and padouk veneer accents. Headstock is capped front and back with Canarywood to match the top. Also featured is a 1.5" brass nut.

The fretboard is a 24 fret beautiful rosewood piece which has Gold Mother of Pearl markers, and brass ringed Turquoise side dots. The spoke wheel truss rod 2-way adjustment is hidden with a gold mother of pearl cover that is held in place with small magnets.

The headstock is the Solano Speed Shop 2+2 design and features Gotoh Y-Key tuning machines. A single round string retainer ensures good nut contact on the A and D strings.

The Bridge is a roller from Schaller and is set at 19mm string spacing. The pickups are DiMarzio Relentless Jazz, and they sound incredible. The electronics feature CTS pots for reliability.

The gold knobs are from StewMac.

item.title: Squier Sonic Precision Bass - Midnight Blue — $269.99
item.description:
$269.99 · Brand New · Haggerty's Music

-YOU ARE VIEWING PHOTOS OF THE ACTUAL BASS BEING SOLD!

This brand new 8.2 lb Squier will ship insured, fully set up, and inspected from Rapid City, SD.

Buy risk free with our 14 day - full refund, less shipping, return policy. Check out our shop policies for more details.

The Squier Sonic Precision Bass delivers the timeless feel and unmistakable punch that have made the Precision Bass one of the most recorded and performed instruments in history. Designed with comfort in mind, its lightweight body and slim neck create an inviting playing experience that’s perfect for beginners while remaining satisfying enough for experienced players looking for a dependable, no-nonsense bass. Whether you’re practicing at home, locking in with a drummer, or playing your first gig, this bass is built to inspire confidence.

Its split single-coil pickup produces the bold, focused tone that has anchored countless songs across rock, country, blues, pop, punk, and everything in between. Thick, supportive lows and punchy mids help every note stand out with clarity, making it easy to find your place in the mix. From smooth fingerstyle grooves to aggressive pick-driven lines, the Sonic Precision Bass delivers the kind of versatile sound that has defined electric bass for generations.

Reliable hardware rounds out a package that’s ready for years of playing, offering dependable tuning stability and accurate intonation every time you plug in. With classic Fender-inspired styling, comfortable playability, and the legendary Precision Bass sound, the Squier Sonic Precision Bass is an outstanding choice for players at any stage of their musical journey. Available now at Haggerty’s Music.

item.title: Dingwall D-Roc Standard 5-String Bass Matte Celestial Blue — $2,545
item.description:
$2,545 · Brand New · Ish Guitars

Dingwall D-Roc Standard 5-String Bass Matte Celestial Blue

Weight: 9.6 lbs 

DESCRIPTION

The D-Roc Standard is a player-driven instrument that sits on the rockier side of the tone tracks, but it’s far from a one-trick pony. With a 4-position Quad-Tone rotary pickup selector that offers four distinct voicings, the D-Roc Standard will help you deliver your own sound to your audience in its purest possible form. 

The body is constructed from an African mahogany called khaya, with a five-piece maple neck and pau ferro fingerboard. Each of the four color options have been finished with a polyurethane coat, making this D-Roc really stand out from the crowd. The growing endorsee list gives an indication of where they’re proving popular – current users include Jermaine Blandford (Jazmine Sullivan), Pete Griffin (Dethklok) and Nick Campbell (Scary Pockets).

The D-Roc Standard is triple-loaded with FD-3n passive pickups. The key to their punchy tone is the use of Neodymium magnets; higher output means a more aggressive sound. Using the 4-position Quad-Tone pickup selector, you can choose to operate just the bridge pickup, the bridge and middle pickups, just the middle pickup, or the bridge, middle and neck pickup in unison. The remaining controls comprise passive tone and master volume.

SPECS

Weight: 9.6lbs 

Year: 2026

Case: Dingwall Padded Gigbag

Body: Khaya

Neck: 5-piece Maple bolt-on

Fretboard: Pau Ferro, 20 ‘banjo-sized’ frets, 9.45-inch radius

Scale Length: 5 string 37–34 inches (940–864mm) 

Pickups: Dingwall FD-3n Pickups 

Electronics: Passive

Bridge: Dingwall ‘Minimalist’ one-piece bridge 

Bridge String Spacing: 18 mm – .709″

Tuners: Open Gear

Nut: Graphtech Black Tusq

SETUP & INSPECTED BY OUR TECH TEAM

All of our guitars come with a FREE professional setup to a comfortable action as specified by that instrument’s manufacturer. Our setups always include inspection of (and necessary adjustment to): action, intonation, tremolo angle, truss rod, frets, nut, bridge, and more. We can accommodate special setup requests - additional charges and/or shipping delays of 1-2 business days could occur.

INDIVIDUALLY PHOTOGRAPHED

After 15 years of innovation in our photography process, Ish Guitars has developed a way to get you the most accurate true-to-life color of every instrument we sell. We never embellish, oversaturate, or misrepresent our images, so that your buying experience can be as much like visiting our showroom as possible. Keep in mind that various computer screens can show colors a bit differently, so the color on your screen may not match the real thing perfectly no matter how hard we try. item.title: Dingwall SP1 4-String Bass Ducati White B-Stock — $2,310
item.description:
$2,310 · Brand New · Ish Guitars

Dingwall SP1 4-String Bass Ducati White B-Stock

Weight: 8.3 lbs 

DESCRIPTION

The SP1 is the latest addition to our ever-expanding Ready To Play range and, as the model name suggests, draws much of its inspiration from the Custom Shop Super P. The initial brief was to come up with a more affordable instrument that combined elements of Leo Fender’s iconic blueprint with some of the innovative features for which Dingwall Guitars are now famous, including the customary multi-scale fingerboard. 

The first major change made to the SP1 was a thinner body profile, resulting in a lightweight instrument that still delivers a solid, resonant tone. The body is crafted from alder, paired with a bolt-on neck made from a single piece of maple. The rosewood fingerboard is flawlessly finished, featuring smoothly rounded edges and 22 banjo-sized frets.

In addition to its multi-scale design, one of the most visually distinctive features of the SP1 is its scratchplate. Originally a hand-painted design by Hugh Fisher, the artwork has since been digitized and UV printed. Each pickguard is slightly transparent, allowing the body color to subtly influence its appearance. Thanks to the transparent material, virtually any color can be printed, offering nearly limitless visual customization.

According to Sheldon Dingwall, “It’s no coincidence that the first bassists to recognize Dingwall’s potential were session players—musicians who play and record bass every day. These professionals need an instrument that’s versatile, studio-ready, comfortable, and easy to use. The SP1 delivers on all fronts, and it’s likely we’ll be seeing a lot more of them in the future.”

The SP1 features a passive electronics setup—no batteries, no onboard EQ—yet its tonal versatility remains exceptional due to high-quality tonewoods, alnico-loaded pickups, and precision construction. The controls are simple: a front volume knob, a rear master tone knob, and the Quad-Tone Pickup Selector located between them. This selector offers four distinct settings: fully anti-clockwise activates the front pickup alone; one click clockwise engages both pickups in series; another click sets them in parallel; and the final position selects the rear pickup by itself.

SPECS

Cosmetic Condition: B-stock condition due to small finish issue by neck pocket. $200 price reduction.

Weight: 8.3 lbs 

Case: Dingwall Padded Gigbag Included

Year: 2026

Made in: Indonesia / Canada (Hybrid Manufacturing)

Body: Alder

Neck: 1-piece Maple, Bolt-On

Fingerboard: Rosewood

Scale Length: 4-String: 32" to 34.25"

Pickups: Dingwall Dual-P (Neck); Dingwall FD3n (Bridge)

Electronics: Passive

Controls: Master volume, 4-Position Quad-Tone pickup selector, Master tone

Bridge: Dingwall ‘Minimalist’ one-piece bridge 

String Spacing at the Bridge:: 19mm (4-string)

Pickup Selector: Position 1: Bridge Solo, Position 2: Bridge/Neck (Parallel), Position 3:  Bridge/Neck (Series), Position 4:Neck Solo

Tuners: Open gear

Nut: Graphtech Black Tusq

SETUP & INSPECTED BY OUR TECH TEAM

All of our guitars come with a FREE professional setup to a comfortable action as specified by that instrument’s manufacturer. Our setups always include inspection of (and necessary adjustment to): action, intonation, tremolo angle, truss rod, frets, nut, bridge, and more. We can accommodate special setup requests - additional charges and/or shipping delays of 1-2 business days could occur.
